summary: Retested Operator Comparison Dashboard after limit=200 → 100 fix. Full UI flow now works end-to-end for admin role. All 6 review checklist items pass: (1) dropdown populates with 7 operators, (2) Carter + Musango → 2 comparison-card-* with all KPIs, (3) daily revenue overlay chart renders, (4) category table renders, (5) 'Add a third operator' + Netflix → 3-column layout, (6) period selector triggers refetch. Required a one-time `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` because Vite was still serving the cached limit=200 bundle even though the source file was updated.

minor.issue: Returns 422 (le=100 not the issue here; backend rejects limit=1 with validation error). Not on the operator-comparison page itself — observed on /admin/admin-dashboard. Pre-existing, out of scope for this review.

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer still logs 'width(-1) and height(-1)' warning on first paint until container measures. Cosmetic only — chart renders correctly after. A minHeight wrapper would silence it.

action_items: Optional polish: wrap ResponsiveContainer in a div with minHeight to remove the recharts width/height warning.
Optional: investigate /api/ratings/?limit=1 422 (pre-existing dashboard issue, not blocking).

Heads-up to main agent: Vite dev server hot-reload did NOT pick up the OperatorComparison.jsx edit until I restarted frontend via supervisor. The first test run after the fix still showed /api/operators/?limit=200 in the network. After restart, requests correctly hit /api/operators/?limit=100. If you see persistent stale-bundle behaviour, a frontend restart resolves it.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correct and minimal — single-line change at OperatorComparison.jsx line 35.

Page still silently swallows operator-catalog errors via `catch { setAllOperators([]); }`. Now that the happy path works, consider surfacing an error toast/banner so future regressions don't silently produce an empty dropdown.
buildCategoryRows pivots correctly; KPI 'leader' (Trophy icon) renders on the operator with max revenue/orders/aov/completion as expected.
Period change correctly triggers refetch (verified: comparison-call count went 3 → 4 with period=7days in query string after switching the selector).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Carter / Musango / Netflix operator IDs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Operator Comparison Dashboard is fully functional. Verified: /api/operators/?limit=100 → 200 OK returns 7 ops; picking 2 ops triggers /api/analytics/admin/operator-comparison → 200 OK; 3-op layout renders; period selector refetches. If you see this page broken in future, first check whether Vite is serving stale code — restart frontend before debugging.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration (202) blocker (limit=200 → 422) was resolved by the main agent's line-35 edit. On first retest run the browser was still requesting limit=200 because Vite's module graph had cached the old version even though hot-reload normally handles single-line edits. After `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` and ~30s wait, the served bundle reflected limit=100, the operators endpoint returned 200, the dropdown populated, and the entire comparison flow (2-op cards → 3-op cards → period refetch) worked end-to-end. No code changes needed from the main agent.
